task_completion.description: Calculate the duration in minutes in a new 'Duration_Mins' column on the 'Trips' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Trips' sheet is visible with a column named 'Duration_Mins'. The cells in this column contain numerical values formatted to one decimal place.

task_completion.description: Calculate 'Total_Starts', 'Total_Ends', and 'Net_Flow' for each station on the 'Station_Summary' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Station_Summary' sheet is visible with columns for 'Total_Starts', 'Total_Ends', and 'Net_Flow' populated with numerical data for the stations.

task_completion.description: Populate the 'Action' column on the 'Station_Summary' sheet based on the 'Net_Flow' values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Station_Summary' sheet shows an 'Action' column containing the text 'Add Bikes', 'Remove Bikes', or 'Monitor' corresponding to the Net_Flow values (-10 or less, 10 or more, and in between, respectively).

task_completion.description: Sort the 'Station_Summary' table by 'Net_Flow' in ascending order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The data in the 'Station_Summary' sheet is ordered such that the 'Net_Flow' column values go from the lowest (most negative) at the top to the highest (most positive) at the bottom.

task_completion.description: Calculate 'Total Trips' and 'Avg Duration (Mins)' for 'Casual' and 'Member' riders on the 'Rider_Summary' sheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Rider_Summary' sheet is visible, showing rows for 'Casual' and 'Member' with calculated numerical values in the 'Total Trips' and 'Avg Duration (Mins)' columns.

task_completion.description: Save the completed analysis to the original file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet application shows no unsaved changes indicator (e.g., no asterisk next to the filename in the title bar, or the save icon is greyed out) after all modifications are complete.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a graphical spreadsheet interface being interacted with, rather than a terminal running a Python/Pandas script to manipulate the Excel file.

integrity.description: The agent used actual spreadsheet formulas rather than hardcoding the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: When cells are selected, the formula bar at the top of the spreadsheet shows formulas (e.g., containing '=', 'COUNTIF', 'SUM', 'IF', or arithmetic operations) instead of static typed numbers.

integrity.description: The agent edited and saved the original file rather than creating a new one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The title bar of the spreadsheet application displays 'bikeshare_q3_data.xlsx' and there is no evidence of a 'Save As' dialog saving the file to a different name or directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected workbook must contain exactly three sheets: 'Trips', 'Station_Summary', and 'Rider_Summary'. The analysis must include the derived columns 'Duration_Mins' (in Trips), 'Net_Flow' (in Station_Summary), and 'Action' (in Station_Summary).
